etymology.description: 穴(穴) (xué, "cave") + 牙(牙) (yá, "tooth") = something boring or piercing through a cave -> to penetrate -> to wear
etymology.interpretation: The character 穿(chuān, "to wear") combines a cave 穴(穴) (xué, "cave") and a tooth 牙(牙) (yá, "tooth"), suggesting the action of piercing or boring through a surface, akin to a tooth digging into material, leading to the concept of passing through or penetrating. This imagery extends to the act of wearing, penetrating clothing.

examples.chinese: 今天我穿了一件新衣服(今天我穿了一件新衣服)。
examples.pinyin: Jīntiān wǒ chuānle yī jiàn xīn yīfú.
examples.english: Today, I wore a new piece of clothing.

examples.chinese: 他每次上班都穿西装(他每次上班都穿西裝)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měicì shàngbān dōu chuān xīzhuāng.
examples.english: He wears a suit every time he goes to work.

examples.chinese: 小孩子喜欢穿鲜艳的颜色(小孩子喜歡穿鮮豔的顏色)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo háizi xǐhuān chuān xiānyàn de yánsè.
examples.english: Children like to wear bright colors.
